results.answer:
The provided documents conflict, so the “current” policy is ambiguous:

- Document A: Refunds may be requested within 14 days; digital goods are non‑refundable; refunds are issued as store credit [A].  
- Document B: Refunds may be requested within 30 days; refunds are issued to the original payment method; digital goods are refundable only if unused; start a refund by contacting support with your order number [B].  
- Additionally, Document E says refunds (when approved) are processed within 5–7 business days and returned to the original payment method [E].

Which policy applies is not specified in the context above.

Refund Policy — §2.1: "Customers may request a refund within 30 days of purchase. Refunds are issued to the original payment method. To start a refund, contact support with your order number." [refund_policy_2026 §2.1]

How long does a refund take under the refund policy? — §1.3: "A refund is processed within 5 to 7 business days after approval. Where does my refund go? Back to the original payment method used for the order." [faq_refunds §1.3]
No — all production deployments require approval from a release manager [Document C].
No — production deployments require release manager approval [deploy_policy_2026 §5.2] and the Approval Matrix specifies production deploys need release manager approval [approval_matrix §A.1].
